rules_evaluated.name: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance
rules_evaluated.note: No instances of honest, delve, landscape (verb), importantly, crucial, substrate, leverage (verb), synergize, paradigm shift found.

rules_evaluated.name: Ritual / practice (noun) / ceremony — hard ban
rules_evaluated.note: No instances of ritual, practice (noun in banned sense), or ceremony found in body.

rules_evaluated.name: No stale markers, session biography
rules_evaluated.note: No 2026- dates, 'caught live', 'compounded', 'restructure', 'extracted from', or (MOVED markers in body.

rules_evaluated.name: Register match — AE101 voice quintet (sponsor context)
rules_evaluated.note: Register is sponsor-appropriate: direct and pragmatic (Boris), frame-based (Martin), Rory reframe at line 23 ('Half the CTOs who fill this worksheet discover…') earns its weight without preach. Voice is conversational and confident, no consultant padding.

rules_evaluated.name: Author-'we' ban
rules_evaluated.note: No first-person-plural referring to training authors. Uses 'you' for sponsor, 'the training' for abstraction.

rules_evaluated.name: Don't smuggle maintainer-vocabulary into body
rules_evaluated.note: No terms-of-art from maintainer notes leaking into body prose. Body stays concrete and operational.

rules_evaluated.name: Over-hedge detector
rules_evaluated.note: Line 20 'Your answer is overridable, not final' is reassurance-shaped but load-bearing for sponsor confidence (pragmatic framing, not apologetic). Sponsor needs to know the commitment is flexible; this serves the audience's actual concern.

rules_evaluated.name: Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy, not student-facing body
rules_evaluated.note: Sponsor-facing asset (not student-facing). No value-prop defensive framing found ('not an aspirational', 'not a vendor', 'unlike consultancy', etc.).

rules_evaluated.name: CTO-repeatable test
rules_evaluated.note: Every major framing is CTO-usable. Lines 18-20 are pragmatic authority framing. No preening phrase-pairs.

rules_evaluated.name: No stylized phrase-pairs
rules_evaluated.note: No Rory two-beat 'X. And Y.' formula-pairs used defensively or as marketing filler. The one Rory beat (line 23) earns its placement.

rules_evaluated.name: Strip strategy-doc vocabulary
rules_evaluated.note: No unearned strategy vocabulary (veto-stakeholder, compounding substrate, authoring move, etc.) in body. Language stays concrete and sponsor-facing.
